cis-alarms.description: Whether to prefix resource names with random prefix
cis-alarms.name: use_random_name_prefix

cis-alarms.description: List of IDs of disabled CIS controls
cis-alarms.name: disabled_controls

cis-alarms.description: Indicates whether or not actions should be executed during any changes to the alarm's state.
cis-alarms.name: actions_enabled

cis-alarms.description: List of ARNs to put as Cloudwatch insuficient data actions (eg, ARN of SNS topic)
cis-alarms.name: insufficient_data_actions

cis-alarms.description: Whether to create the Cloudwatch log metric filter and metric alarms
cis-alarms.name: create

cis-alarms.description: A name prefix for the cloudwatch alarm (if use_random_name_prefix is true, this will be ignored)
cis-alarms.name: name_prefix

cis-alarms.description: A map of overrides to apply to each control
cis-alarms.name: control_overrides

cis-alarms.description: The namespace where metric filter and metric alarm should be cleated
cis-alarms.name: namespace

cis-alarms.description: The name of the log group to associate the metric filter with
cis-alarms.name: log_group_name

cis-alarms.description: List of ARNs to put as Cloudwatch Alarms actions (eg, ARN of SNS topic)
cis-alarms.name: alarm_actions

cis-alarms.description: A mapping of tags to assign to all resources
cis-alarms.name: tags

cis-alarms.description: List of ARNs to put as Cloudwatch OK actions (eg, ARN of SNS topic)
cis-alarms.name: ok_actions

composite-alarm.description: Whether to create the Cloudwatch composite alarm
composite-alarm.name: create

composite-alarm.description: A map of actions suppressor alarm configurations.
composite-alarm.name: actions_suppressor

composite-alarm.description: The set of actions to execute when this alarm transitions into an INSUFFICIENT_DATA state from any other state. Each action is specified as an Amazon Resource Name (ARN). Up to 5 actions are allowed.
composite-alarm.name: insufficient_data_actions

composite-alarm.description: The set of actions to execute when this alarm transitions into an OK state from any other state. Each action is specified as an Amazon Resource Name (ARN). Up to 5 actions are allowed.
composite-alarm.name: ok_actions

composite-alarm.description: An expression that specifies which other alarms are to be evaluated to determine this composite alarm's state. The maximum length is 10240 characters.
composite-alarm.name: alarm_rule

composite-alarm.description: The descriptive name for the composite alarm. This name must be unique within the region.
composite-alarm.name: alarm_name

composite-alarm.description: The description for the composite alarm.
composite-alarm.name: alarm_description

composite-alarm.description: Indicates whether or not actions should be executed during any changes to the composite alarm's state. Defaults to true.
composite-alarm.name: actions_enabled

composite-alarm.description: The set of actions to execute when this alarm transitions into an ALARM state from any other state. Each action is specified as an Amazon Resource Name (ARN). Up to 5 actions are allowed.
composite-alarm.name: alarm_actions

composite-alarm.description: A mapping of tags to assign to all resources
composite-alarm.name: tags

log-account-policy.description: The name of the log group under which the log stream is to be created.
log-account-policy.name: log_account_policy_name

log-account-policy.description: Currently defaults to and only accepts the value: ALL.
log-account-policy.name: log_account_policy_scope

log-account-policy.description: Specifies the data protection policy in JSON.
log-account-policy.name: policy_document

log-account-policy.description: The name of the data protection policy document.
log-account-policy.name: log_data_protection_policy_name

log-account-policy.description: The description of the data protection policy document.
log-account-policy.name: log_data_protection_description

log-account-policy.description: Criteria for applying a subscription filter policy to a selection of log groups. The only allowable criteria selector is LogGroupName NOT IN [].
log-account-policy.name: log_account_policy_selection_criteria

log-account-policy.description: Type of account policy. Either DATA_PROTECTION_POLICY or SUBSCRIPTION_FILTER_POLICY. You can have one account policy per type in an account.
log-account-policy.name: log_account_policy_type

log-account-policy.description: Set of at least 1 sensitive data identifiers that you want to mask.
log-account-policy.name: data_identifiers

log-account-policy.description: Configures CloudWatch Logs as a findings destination.
log-account-policy.name: findings_destination_cloudwatch_log_group

log-account-policy.description: Configures Kinesis Firehose as a findings destination.
log-account-policy.name: findings_destination_firehose_delivery_stream

log-account-policy.description: Configures S3 as a findings destination.
log-account-policy.name: findings_destination_s3_bucket

log-account-policy.description: Whether to create the cloudwatch log data protection policy.
log-account-policy.name: create

log-account-policy.description: Whether to create the cloudwatch log data protection policy.
log-account-policy.name: create_log_data_protection_policy

log-anomaly-detector.description: Optionally assigns a AWS KMS key to secure this anomaly detector and its findings. If a key is assigned, the anomalies found and the model used by this detector are encrypted at rest with the key. If a key is assigned to an anomaly detector, a user must have permissions for both this key and for the anomaly detector to retrieve information about the anomalies that it finds.
log-anomaly-detector.name: kms_key_id

log-anomaly-detector.description: Whether to create the anomaly detector.
log-anomaly-detector.name: create

log-anomaly-detector.description: Array containing the ARN of the log group that this anomaly detector will watch. You can specify only one log group ARN.
log-anomaly-detector.name: log_group_arns

log-anomaly-detector.description: Number of days to have visibility on an anomaly. After this time period has elapsed for an anomaly, it will be automatically baselined and the anomaly detector will treat new occurrences of a similar anomaly as normal. Therefore, if you do not correct the cause of an anomaly during the time period specified in anomaly_visibility_time, it will be considered normal going forward and will not be detected as an anomaly. Valid Range: Minimum value of 7. Maximum value of 90.
log-anomaly-detector.name: anomaly_visibility_time

log-anomaly-detector.description: Whether or not to enable the anomaly detector.
log-anomaly-detector.name: enabled

log-anomaly-detector.description: Specifies how often the anomaly detector is to run and look for anomalies. Set this value according to the frequency that the log group receives new logs. For example, if the log group receives new log events every 10 minutes, then 15 minutes might be a good setting for evaluation_frequency. Valid Values: ONE_MIN | FIVE_MIN | TEN_MIN | FIFTEEN_MIN | THIRTY_MIN | ONE_HOUR.
log-anomaly-detector.name: evaluation_frequency

log-anomaly-detector.description: You can use this parameter to limit the anomaly detection model to examine only log events that match the pattern you specify here.
log-anomaly-detector.name: filter_pattern

log-data-protection-policy.description: The name of the log group under which the log stream is to be created.
log-data-protection-policy.name: log_group_name

log-data-protection-policy.description: The name of the data protection policy document.
log-data-protection-policy.name: log_data_protection_policy_name

log-data-protection-policy.description: The description of the data protection policy document.
log-data-protection-policy.name: log_data_protection_description

log-data-protection-policy.description: Configures CloudWatch Logs as a findings destination.
log-data-protection-policy.name: findings_destination_cloudwatch_log_group

log-data-protection-policy.description: Configures Kinesis Firehose as a findings destination.
log-data-protection-policy.name: findings_destination_firehose_delivery_stream

log-data-protection-policy.description: Whether to create the cloudwatch log data protection policy.
log-data-protection-policy.name: create

log-data-protection-policy.description: Whether to create the cloudwatch log data protection policy.
log-data-protection-policy.name: create_log_data_protection_policy

log-data-protection-policy.description: Specifies the data protection policy in JSON.
log-data-protection-policy.name: policy_document

log-data-protection-policy.description: Set of at least 1 sensitive data identifiers that you want to mask.
log-data-protection-policy.name: data_identifiers

log-data-protection-policy.description: Configures S3 as a findings destination.
log-data-protection-policy.name: findings_destination_s3_bucket

log-group.description: Specified the log class of the log group. Possible values are: STANDARD or INFREQUENT_ACCESS
log-group.name: log_group_class

log-group.description: Set to true if you do not wish the log group (and any logs it may contain) to be deleted at destroy time, and instead just remove the log group from the Terraform state
log-group.name: skip_destroy

log-group.description: A map of tags to add to Cloudwatch log group
log-group.name: tags

log-group.description: Whether to create the Cloudwatch log group
log-group.name: create

log-group.description: A name for the log group
log-group.name: name

log-group.description: A name prefix for the log group
log-group.name: name_prefix

log-group.description: Specifies the number of days you want to retain log events in the specified log group. Possible values are: 0, 1, 3, 5, 7, 14, 30, 60, 90, 120, 150, 180, 365, 400, 545, 731, 1096, 1827, 2192, 2557, 2922, 3288 and 3653.
log-group.name: retention_in_days

log-group.description: The ARN of the KMS Key to use when encrypting logs
log-group.name: kms_key_id

log-metric-filter.description: The name of the CloudWatch metric to which the monitored log information should be published (e.g. ErrorCount)
log-metric-filter.name: metric_transformation_name

log-metric-filter.description: The value to emit when a filter pattern does not match a log event. Conflicts with `metric_transformation_dimensions`.
log-metric-filter.name: metric_transformation_default_value

log-metric-filter.description: Whether to create the Cloudwatch log metric filter
log-metric-filter.name: create_cloudwatch_log_metric_filter

log-metric-filter.description: The destination namespace of the CloudWatch metric.
log-metric-filter.name: metric_transformation_namespace

log-metric-filter.description: What to publish to the metric. For example, if you're counting the occurrences of a particular term like 'Error', the value will be '1' for each occurrence. If you're counting the bytes transferred the published value will be the value in the log event.
log-metric-filter.name: metric_transformation_value

log-metric-filter.description: The unit to assign to the metric. If you omit this, the unit is set as None.
log-metric-filter.name: metric_transformation_unit

log-metric-filter.description: The additional dimensions to assign to the metric, in the form `"name" = "$.value". Conflicts with `metric_transformation_default_value`.
log-metric-filter.name: metric_transformation_dimensions

log-metric-filter.description: A name for the metric filter.
log-metric-filter.name: name

log-metric-filter.description: A valid CloudWatch Logs filter pattern for extracting metric data out of ingested log events.
log-metric-filter.name: pattern

log-metric-filter.description: The name of the log group to associate the metric filter with
log-metric-filter.name: log_group_name

log-stream.description: Whether to create the Cloudwatch log stream
log-stream.name: create

log-stream.description: A name for the log stream
log-stream.name: name

log-stream.description: A name of the log group
log-stream.name: log_group_name

log-subscription-filter.description: A valid CloudWatch Logs filter pattern for subscribing to a filtered stream of log events. Use empty string to match everything
log-subscription-filter.name: filter_pattern

log-subscription-filter.description: The name of the log group to associate the subscription filter with
log-subscription-filter.name: log_group_name

log-subscription-filter.description: The ARN of an IAM role that grants Amazon CloudWatch Logs permissions to deliver ingested log events to the destination
log-subscription-filter.name: role_arn

log-subscription-filter.description: The method used to distribute log data to the destination. By default log data is grouped by log stream, but the grouping can be set to random for a more even distribution
log-subscription-filter.name: distribution

log-subscription-filter.description: Whether to create the Cloudwatch log stream
log-subscription-filter.name: create

log-subscription-filter.description: A name for the log stream
log-subscription-filter.name: name

log-subscription-filter.description: The ARN of the destination to deliver matching log events to. Kinesis stream or Lambda function ARN
log-subscription-filter.name: destination_arn

metric-alarm.description: The period in seconds over which the specified statistic is applied.
metric-alarm.name: period

metric-alarm.description: Used only for alarms based on percentiles. If you specify ignore, the alarm state will not change during periods with too few data points to be statistically significant. If you specify evaluate or omit this parameter, the alarm will always be evaluated and possibly change state no matter how many data points are available. The following values are supported: ignore, and evaluate.
metric-alarm.name: evaluate_low_sample_count_percentiles

metric-alarm.description: Enables you to create an alarm based on a metric math expression. You may specify at most 20.
metric-alarm.name: metric_query

metric-alarm.description: A mapping of tags to assign to all resources
metric-alarm.name: tags

metric-alarm.description: If this is an alarm based on an anomaly detection model, make this value match the ID of the ANOMALY_DETECTION_BAND function.
metric-alarm.name: threshold_metric_id

metric-alarm.description: The unit for the alarm's associated metric.
metric-alarm.name: unit

metric-alarm.description: The namespace for the alarm's associated metric. See docs for the list of namespaces. See docs for supported metrics.
metric-alarm.name: namespace

metric-alarm.description: The list of actions to execute when this alarm transitions into an INSUFFICIENT_DATA state from any other state. Each action is specified as an Amazon Resource Name (ARN).
metric-alarm.name: insufficient_data_actions

metric-alarm.description: The list of actions to execute when this alarm transitions into an OK state from any other state. Each action is specified as an Amazon Resource Name (ARN).
metric-alarm.name: ok_actions

metric-alarm.description: Sets how this alarm is to handle missing data points. The following values are supported: missing, ignore, breaching and notBreaching.
metric-alarm.name: treat_missing_data

metric-alarm.description: The statistic to apply to the alarm's associated metric. Either of the following is supported: SampleCount, Average, Sum, Minimum, Maximum
metric-alarm.name: statistic

metric-alarm.description: The list of actions to execute when this alarm transitions into an ALARM state from any other state. Each action is specified as an Amazon Resource Name (ARN).
metric-alarm.name: alarm_actions

metric-alarm.description: The number of datapoints that must be breaching to trigger the alarm.
metric-alarm.name: datapoints_to_alarm

metric-alarm.description: Whether to create the Cloudwatch metric alarm
metric-alarm.name: create_metric_alarm

metric-alarm.description: The description for the alarm.
metric-alarm.name: alarm_description

metric-alarm.description: The arithmetic operation to use when comparing the specified Statistic and Threshold. The specified Statistic value is used as the first operand. Either of the following is supported: GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold, GreaterThanThreshold, LessThanThreshold, LessThanOrEqualToThreshold.
metric-alarm.name: comparison_operator

metric-alarm.description: The number of periods over which data is compared to the specified threshold.
metric-alarm.name: evaluation_periods

metric-alarm.description: Indicates whether or not actions should be executed during any changes to the alarm's state. Defaults to true.
metric-alarm.name: actions_enabled

metric-alarm.description: The dimensions for the alarm's associated metric.
metric-alarm.name: dimensions

metric-alarm.description: The percentile statistic for the metric associated with the alarm. Specify a value between p0.0 and p100.
metric-alarm.name: extended_statistic

metric-alarm.description: The descriptive name for the alarm. This name must be unique within the user's AWS account.
metric-alarm.name: alarm_name

metric-alarm.description: The value against which the specified statistic is compared.
metric-alarm.name: threshold

metric-alarm.description: The name for the alarm's associated metric. See docs for supported metrics.
metric-alarm.name: metric_name

metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.description: Whether to create the Cloudwatch metric alarm
metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.name: create_metric_alarm

metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.description: The descriptive name for the alarm. This name must be unique within the user's AWS account.
metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.name: alarm_name

metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.description: The list of actions to execute when this alarm transitions into an ALARM state from any other state. Each action is specified as an Amazon Resource Name (ARN).
metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.name: alarm_actions

metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.description: The list of actions to execute when this alarm transitions into an INSUFFICIENT_DATA state from any other state. Each action is specified as an Amazon Resource Name (ARN).
metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.name: insufficient_data_actions

metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.description: The unit for the alarm's associated metric.
metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.name: unit

metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.description: The namespace for the alarm's associated metric. See docs for the list of namespaces. See docs for supported metrics.
metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.name: namespace

metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.description: The period in seconds over which the specified statistic is applied.
metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.name: period

metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.description: The dimensions for the alarm's associated metric.
metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.name: dimensions

metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.description: The percentile statistic for the metric associated with the alarm. Specify a value between p0.0 and p100.
metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.name: extended_statistic

metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.description: Delimiter between alarm name and dimension key. Use dash or underscore for pretty alert names.
metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.name: alarm_name_delimiter

metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.description: The arithmetic operation to use when comparing the specified Statistic and Threshold. The specified Statistic value is used as the first operand. Either of the following is supported: GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold, GreaterThanThreshold, LessThanThreshold, LessThanOrEqualToThreshold.
metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.name: comparison_operator

metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.description: The number of periods over which data is compared to the specified threshold.
metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.name: evaluation_periods

metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.description: The value against which the specified statistic is compared.
metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.name: threshold

metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.description: The name for the alarm's associated metric. See docs for supported metrics.
metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.name: metric_name

metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.description: The list of actions to execute when this alarm transitions into an OK state from any other state. Each action is specified as an Amazon Resource Name (ARN).
metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.name: ok_actions

metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.description: Sets how this alarm is to handle missing data points. The following values are supported: missing, ignore, breaching and notBreaching.
metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.name: treat_missing_data

metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.description: Enables you to create an alarm based on a metric math expression. You may specify at most 20.
metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.name: metric_query

metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.description: The description for the alarm.
metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.name: alarm_description

metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.description: The statistic to apply to the alarm's associated metric. Either of the following is supported: SampleCount, Average, Sum, Minimum, Maximum
metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.name: statistic

metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.description: Indicates whether or not actions should be executed during any changes to the alarm's state. Defaults to true.
metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.name: actions_enabled

metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.description: The number of datapoints that must be breaching to trigger the alarm.
metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.name: datapoints_to_alarm

metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.description: Used only for alarms based on percentiles. If you specify ignore, the alarm state will not change during periods with too few data points to be statistically significant. If you specify evaluate or omit this parameter, the alarm will always be evaluated and possibly change state no matter how many data points are available. The following values are supported: ignore, and evaluate.
metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.name: evaluate_low_sample_count_percentiles

metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.description: A mapping of tags to assign to all resources
metric-alarms-by-multiple-dimensions.name: tags

metric-stream.description: Whether to create the Cloudwatch metric stream.
metric-stream.name: create

metric-stream.description: Friendly name of the metric stream. If omitted, Terraform will assign a random, unique name. Conflicts with name_prefix.
metric-stream.name: name

metric-stream.description: ARN of the Amazon Kinesis Firehose delivery stream to use for this metric stream.
metric-stream.name: firehose_arn

metric-stream.description: ARN of the IAM role that this metric stream will use to access Amazon Kinesis Firehose resources.
metric-stream.name: role_arn

metric-stream.description: Map of exclusive metric filters. If you specify this parameter, the stream sends metrics from all metric namespaces except for the namespaces and the conditional metric names that you specify here. If you don't specify metric names or provide empty metric names whole metric namespace is excluded. Conflicts with include_filter.
metric-stream.name: exclude_filter

metric-stream.description: Map of inclusive metric filters. If you specify this parameter, the stream sends only the conditional metric names from the metric namespaces that you specify here. If you don't specify metric names or provide empty metric names whole metric namespace is included. Conflicts with exclude_filter.
metric-stream.name: include_filter

metric-stream.description: Creates a unique friendly name beginning with the specified prefix. Conflicts with name.
metric-stream.name: name_prefix

metric-stream.description: Output format for the stream. Possible values are json, opentelemetry0.7, and opentelemetry1.0
metric-stream.name: output_format

metric-stream.description: A map of tags to add to the Cloudwatch Metric Stream.
metric-stream.name: tags

query-definition.description: Whether to create the Cloudwatch query definition
query-definition.name: create

query-definition.description: Specific log groups to use with the query.
query-definition.name: log_group_names

examples:
metric_query = [{
    id = "e1"

    return_data = true
    expression  = "TIME_SERIES(1)"
    label       = "deployment"
    period      = 60
  }]
